title: 命案目睹记
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\136-命案目睹记-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Lucy Espero arrives at the estate to begin her role as a housekeeper. She meets Emma Crickenshaw, who explains that she requires an active worker rather than a simple cleaner. Emma notes that she and her ailing father live a modest lifestyle on the estate, supported by two other staff members: Mrs. Kid, who works in the mornings, and Mrs. Hart, who polishes brass three days a week. To justify her employment, Lucy claims she accepted a lower wage than she previously commanded with the Kennedy family to be closer to her sick aunt in Bletchley. Emma agrees to give Lucy afternoons off, provided she returns by six in the evening.

Emma introduces Lucy to her father, Mr. Crickensopp, a tall, thin man in a wheelchair with a suspicious, bulldog-like face. He is irritable and warns Lucy that despite the size of the house, the family is not wealthy. He expresses a fierce desire to keep the land around the house and refuses to leave. After the meeting, Emma shows Lucy her spacious but dimly lit bedroom and the kitchen.

The following morning, Lucy completes her chores and speaks with Mrs. Kid, a petite woman with sharp insight. Mrs. Kid reveals details about the family: the eldest son Edmund was killed in the war; the second son Cecil lives abroad; the third son Harold lives in London; and the fourth son Alfred is a troublemaker. The deceased daughter, Edith, left behind a husband, Brian, and a son, Alexander, who visits during school breaks. After Mrs. Kid leaves, Lucy cleans the kitchen and prepares afternoon tea before heading out to explore the area.
